name: products
title: Products
description: Sellable items: physical goods, digital downloads, services, subscriptions, and depreciable assets share this one schema via product_type. v1 is catalog data only -- orders, order lines, and stock levels are a later slice (see dbbasic-package.json's description, reconciled against a private predecessor-system catalog audit); price_cents/cost_cents/salvage_value_cents are integer cents, never a float or currency type.

fields.name: product_type
fields.label: Type
fields.help: Matches the predecessor system's Product model type choices (reconciled against a private source audit, not part of this repo). asset carries the depreciation fields below; behavior (a monthly depreciation schedule) is not built here, only the fields.

fields.name: income_account
fields.label: Income Account
fields.help: The real predecessor links to a finance Account (income side). No finance package exists yet in this codebase -- carried as a plain text account id so a future app-finance package has somewhere to point without a backfill.

fields.name: expense_account
fields.label: Expense Account
fields.help: Same provenance as income_account, expense side.

fields.name: digital_file_id
fields.label: Digital File
fields.help: Pointer to the downloadable file for a digital product. No enforced relation to app-files yet (this package has no dependency on it) -- carried as a plain text id so a future digital-delivery slice has somewhere to point without a backfill.

fields.name: useful_life_months
fields.label: Useful Life (months)
fields.help: ASSET parity field, carried from the predecessor system's depreciation fields (reconciled against a private source audit, not part of this repo). Only meaningful when product_type is asset; depreciation math itself is not built here.

fields.name: salvage_value_cents
fields.label: Salvage Value (cents)
fields.help: ASSET parity field. Integer cents, same doctrine as price_cents/cost_cents.

fields.name: depreciation_method
fields.label: Depreciation Method
fields.help: ASSET parity field. No default -- blank means not an asset, or an asset whose method has not been set yet; left unset rather than forced to a guessed default.

fields.name: asset_status
fields.label: Asset Status
fields.help: ASSET parity field. Kept as plain text (not an enum) in v1: the predecessor's asset disposal states are a later slice's concern, not reconstructed here.
